x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces DYCD's program finder and open supply data (programs, program sites, providers, contracts, service areas, aggregate participant demographics) and adds apply_to_program — the net-new write workflow that today lives only in DYCD's seasonal online application flow and the DYCD Connect / DiscoverDYCD apps.

server.name: io.nyc.dycd
server.title: NYC Youth & Community Development (DYCD)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Department of Youth and Community Development — find programs (SYEP, COMPASS, Beacon, and more), their delivery sites and the community-based providers that run them, the contracts that fund them, Neighborhood Development Areas, and aggregate participant demographics from NYC Open Data and the DiscoverDYCD finder, plus the net-new ability to apply to a program (e.g. an SYEP application) that today lives only in DYCD's seasonal application flow.
server.instructions: Use find_programs to discover DYCD program offerings by area, type, applicant age, or borough, and get_program for one offering. find_program_sites / get_program_site locate the physical sites where a program is delivered (with slots, participants, provider, and geography). find_providers covers the contracted CBOs; find_contracts the funding behind them; find_service_areas the Neighborhood Development Areas. find_participant_demographics returns AGGREGATE demographics only — DYCD never exposes individual participants. list_my_applications reads an authenticated applicant's own applications. apply_to_program (net-new) submits a program application; it requires an authenticated applicant and mutates state — confirm the program, applicant details (and a guardian for minors) with the user before submitting.

tools.name: find_programs
tools.title: Find Programs
tools.description: Search DYCD program offerings (SYEP, COMPASS/SONYC, Beacon, Cornerstone, adult literacy, immigrant services) by program area, type, applicant age, borough, or free text.

tools.name: get_program
tools.title: Get Program
tools.description: Fetch a single DYCD program offering (eligibility, age range, how to apply) by its program-type key.

tools.name: find_program_sites
tools.title: Find Program Sites
tools.description: Search DYCD program sites by borough, program area/type, provider, council district, or NDA — with slots, participants, and geography.

tools.name: get_program_site
tools.title: Get Program Site
tools.description: Fetch a DYCD program site (provider, program, slots, participants, address, geography) by PortfolioID.

tools.name: find_providers
tools.title: Find Providers
tools.description: Search the community-based organizations DYCD contracts to run programs, by program area, fiscal year, or free text.

tools.name: find_contracts
tools.title: Find Contracts
tools.description: Search DYCD contracts by provider, major program, status, or fiscal year — the funding behind program sites.

tools.name: find_service_areas
tools.title: Find Service Areas
tools.description: List DYCD Neighborhood Development Areas (NDAs) — the community-development catchments — by borough.

tools.name: find_participant_demographics
tools.title: Find Participant Demographics
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE participant demographics (race/ethnicity, gender identity) by geography or program type. Never returns individual participants.

tools.name: list_my_applications
tools.title: List My Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated applicant's own DYCD program applications and their status — today visible only inside DYCD's seasonal application flow.

tools.name: apply_to_program
tools.title: Apply to a Program
tools.description: Submit a DYCD program application (e.g. an SYEP application). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in DYCD's seasonal online application flow and the DYCD Connect / DiscoverDYCD apps. Requires an authenticated applicant. Mutates state — confirm the program and applicant details (and a guardian for minors) first.
